description: Implement `nax unlock` CLI command to safely release a stale execution lock (nax.lock) without requiring manual filesystem access.

userStories.title: nax unlock command releases stale lock safely
userStories.description:
Add a `nax unlock` CLI command to bin/nax.ts and implement its logic in src/commands/unlock.ts.

Existing patterns to follow:
- Look at src/commands/precheck.ts for a complete command example (imports, structure, Commander wiring)
- Look at src/execution/lock.ts for acquireLock/releaseLock/isProcessAlive logic
- Look at test/unit/commands/ for existing unit test examples (e.g. test/unit/commands/precheck.test.ts)
- Tests go in: test/unit/commands/unlock.test.ts
- Test runner: `bun test --no-coverage test/unit/commands/unlock.test.ts`

The command must:
- Read nax.lock and verify the holding process is actually dead before removing
- Refuse to unlock if the lock-holding process is still alive (active run)
- Print the lock state (PID, age, alive/dead) before acting
- Accept `--force` flag to skip the liveness check and remove unconditionally
- Accept `-d <dir>` / `--dir <dir>` flag to target a specific project directory (defaults to cwd)
- Exit 0 on success, non-zero on failure
- Export an `unlockCommand` function and wire into bin/nax.ts program
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `nax unlock` with no lock file prints 'No lock file found' and exits 0
AC2: `nax unlock` when lock PID is alive prints error 'nax is still running (PID <n>). Use --force to override.' and exits 1 without deleting lock
AC3: `nax unlock` when lock PID is dead prints lock info (PID, age in minutes) then removes nax.lock and exits 0
AC4: `nax unlock --force` removes nax.lock unconditionally regardless of process state and exits 0
AC5: `nax unlock -d <path>` targets the specified directory (not cwd)
AC6: Unit tests in test/unit/commands/unlock.test.ts cover all four scenarios (no lock, alive, dead, force)
